rationale: The citing paper discusses Jap and Ganesan (2000) multiple times as a key prior B2B study on relationship drivers and life-cycle contingency, and it gives the paper a dedicated summary row in its literature table. It uses that prior work to frame the gap that existing B2B evidence is often survey-based and limited in capturing dynamic, time-varying effects in observed behavior data. This is more than a passing citation because it materially supports the conceptual positioning and motivation for the citing paper's dynamic modeling contribution.
relation_description: Kim and Kumar treat Jap and Ganesan (2000) as part of the conceptual and empirical foundation showing that relationship mechanisms can have stage-contingent effects in B2B settings. They build from that foundation by proposing a longitudinal, transaction-data model of dynamic economic versus relational communication effects.
